appearance.tooltip.custom_colors: Click on a color to open the color picker. Changes are applied immediately.
appearance.tooltip.reset_themes: Select a preset theme or customize individual colors below.
appearance.tooltip.reset_to_default_dark: Reset all colors to the default dark theme.
cursor_trail.sprite_path: Custom sprite PNG (leave empty for default dot):
cursor_trail.tooltip.blend_mode: Alpha = solid stamps (ink). Additive = stamps brighten what they overlap (glow).
cursor_trail.tooltip.enabled: Draws a trail of stamps following the mouse cursor. Follows the real or fake cursor, whichever is visible.
cursor_trail.tooltip.only_on_my_screen: When enabled, the cursor trail will not be captured by OBS.
cursor_trail.tooltip.only_on_obs: When enabled, the cursor trail is only visible in OBS, not on your screen.
cursor_trail.tooltip.sprite_path: Absolute path to a PNG file (max 256x256). If empty or invalid, a soft white dot is used.
cursor_trail.tooltip.stamp_spacing: Distance between stamps in pixels. Smaller = denser trail. Density stays constant regardless of mouse speed or framerate.
cursor_trail.tooltip.tail_size_scale: Size of the oldest visible stamp relative to the newest. 1.0 = constant size, 0.0 = tail tapers to a point.
cursor_trail.tooltip.use_gradient: When enabled, stamps near the cursor use the head color and fade to the tail color as they age.
cursor_trail.tooltip.use_velocity_size: Stamps emitted while moving the cursor fast are larger than stamps emitted while moving slowly. Each stamp keeps its size for its whole lifetime.
eyezoom.tooltip:
Clone Width controls how wide the EyeZoom clone samples.
Overlay Pixels controls how much of the numbered overlay is drawn on each side of center.
game_state.title: Title Screen
general.sens.tooltip.eyezoom_sensitivity:
EyeZoom mouse sensitivity multiplier (1.0 = normal).
Overrides global sensitivity when in EyeZoom.
general.sens.tooltip.global_sensitivity:
Global mouse sensitivity multiplier (1.0 = normal).
Affects all modes unless overridden.
general.sens.tooltip.preemptive_sensitiv:
Preemptive mouse sensitivity multiplier (1.0 = normal).
Overrides global sensitivity when in Preemptive.
general.tooltip.go_borderless:
Instantly toggles the game window into borderless mode.
If already borderless, it returns to windowed mode.
general.tooltip.mirrors: Assign mirrors and mirror groups to modes
hotkeys.allow_exit_to_default_mode_regar: Allow exit to default mode regardless of game state
hotkeys.bind_hotkey.tooltip.prompt: Press a key or key combination.
hotkeys.delete_hotkey.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this hotkey?
hotkeys.delete_sens_hotkey_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this sensitivity hotkey?
hotkeys.reset_warning: This will delete ALL custom hotkeys and restore the default hotkeys.
hotkeys.sens.tooltip.bind_hotkey: Click to bind a key combination for this sensitivity override.
hotkeys.sens.tooltip.separate_xy: Enable to set different sensitivity values for X and Y axes.
hotkeys.sens.tooltip.toggle: When enabled, pressing the hotkey again resets sensitivity back to normal (mode override or global sensitivity).
hotkeys.tooltip.allow_exit_to_default_mo:
When enabled, toggling BACK to your default mode (%s) is allowed even if required game states are not met.
Toggling INTO the target mode still requires the configured game state.
hotkeys.tooltip.block_key_from_game:
When enabled, the key event that matches this hotkey is consumed and will NOT be forwarded to the game.
The hotkey will still trigger normally.
hotkeys.tooltip.default_mode_toggle_back: Your default mode (%s) is the implicit toggle-back mode
hotkeys.tooltip.mode_hotkey: Pressing this hotkey toggles between your default mode (%s) and this mode
hotkeys.tooltip.sensitivity_hotkeys:
Sensitivity hotkeys temporarily override mouse sensitivity.
The override persists until the next mode change.
hotkeys.tooltip.trigger_on_release: When checked, the hotkey triggers when the key is released instead of pressed
hotkeys.tooltip.trigger_only_when_holdin: When checked, the mode activates while the key is held and reverts to default when released
hotkeys.tooltip.warning_wpstateout: State-based hotkey restrictions are currently disabled, so hotkeys will trigger regardless of required game states. Install the State Output mod to enable these conditions.
images.delete_images_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete image '{}'?
images.name: Name
images.reset_warning: This will delete ALL custom images and restore the default images.
images.tooltip: You can click and drag images in the game window to move them while this tab is open
images.tooltip.border: Draw a border around the image overlay.
images.tooltip.relative_sizing: When enabled, size this image from the cropped source size using the scale slider. When disabled, width and height stretch the image to an exact size.
images.tooltip.only_on_my_screen: When enabled, this image will only be visible to you and not captured by OBS
inputs.bind_from_key.tooltip.prompt: Press a key to bind as INPUT.
inputs.bind_output_scan.tooltip.prompt: Press a key to set OUTPUT Scan Code.
inputs.bind_output_vk.tooltip.prompt: Press a key to set OUTPUT Virtual Key Code.
inputs.bind_text_override_vk.tooltip.pro: Press a key to set TEXT OVERRIDE Virtual Key Code.
inputs.configure_cursors_for_different_g: Configure cursors for different game states:
inputs.tooltip.default_indicator_image: Built-in default image. Click to choose a custom image.
inputs.tooltip.show_rebind_indicator: Shows an icon on screen to indicate the current rebind state.
inputs.tooltip.physical_layout:
Physical keyboard layout.
ANSI: US-style, common in North America.
ISO: European-style, has extra key next to LShift.
Auto: detects based on Windows language.
inputs.tooltip.key_labels:
Key labels to display on the keyboard.
Choose the layout that matches your physical keyboard.
inputs.keyboard_layout_state_note: Default is the fallback layout. Cursor Free and Cursor Grabbed only override it while the cursor matches that state.
inputs.keyboard_layout_tip: Tip: Right-click a key to configure it for the selected rebind layout. Use Add Custom Bind below for keys that are not shown.
inputs.reset_cursors_to_defaults_tip: This will reset all cursor settings to their default values.
inputs.tooltip.configure_key_rebinds: Configure key rebinds in the Keyboard Layout window (right-click keys).
inputs.tooltip.enable_key_rebinding: When enabled, configured key rebinds will intercept keyboard input and send the remapped key to the game instead.
inputs.tooltip.allow_system_alt_f4: Keeps Alt+F4 reserved for Windows when the physical Alt key is held, even if Alt or F4 is rebound.
inputs.tooltip.allow_system_alt_tab: Keeps Alt+Tab reserved for Windows when the physical Alt key is held, even if Alt or Tab is rebound.
inputs.tooltip.suppress_caps_lock_toggle: Stops Caps Lock from toggling, but Minecraft still receives the key press.
inputs.tooltip.use_system_key_repeat:
When enabled, Toolscreen forwards whatever keydown and keyup events Windows provides without changing the system key repeat settings.
When disabled, Toolscreen ignores physical auto-repeat keydowns and repeats keys locally so rebinds stay consistent. Only the most recently pressed key can repeat.
inputs.tooltip.enter_unicode_or_codepoin: Enter a Unicode character or codepoint:
inputs.tooltip.key_rebinding: Intercept keyboard inputs and remap them before they reach the game.
inputs.tooltip.key_repeat_delay:
Only used when system key repeat is disabled.
Time between repeated key presses while held.
-1 = Auto (5ms), 1-50ms = custom delay in 1ms steps.
Only applied while the game window is focused.
inputs.tooltip.key_repeat_start_delay:
Only used when system key repeat is disabled.
Delay before a held key starts repeating.
-1 = Auto (200ms), 100-300ms = custom delay.
Only applied while the game window is focused.
inputs.tooltip.keyboard_layout_scale: Visual scale for the keyboard layout preview only.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_layout_state: Choose which rebind layout you are editing.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_layout_default_but: Default layout: used unless a cursor-specific override matches.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_layout_cursor_free: Cursor Free layout: used while the cursor is visible and free.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_layout_cursor_grab: Cursor Grabbed layout: used while the cursor is hidden or grabbed.
inputs.tooltip.open_keyboard_layout:
Shows a visual keyboard. Keys with a configured rebind are outlined.
Each key shows the output scan code (large text) and the game keybind hotkey below it.
Physical key labels are hidden in this view.
Use the layout buttons to edit Default, Cursor Free, and Cursor Grabbed variants.
Right-click a key to configure rebinds.
Use Add Custom Bind below for keys that are not shown in the layout.
inputs.tooltip.add_custom_bind:
Press a key to add an extra source button below the keyboard layout.
If that key is already shown above, use its existing button instead.
Right-click a custom key to configure it, change its input key, or remove it.
inputs.tooltip.custom_key_input: Click the input button to capture a new source key immediately, or use Pick to browse the key list. Changing it also moves any saved rebinds for this custom key.
inputs.tooltip.custom_layout_key: Right-click to configure rebinds, change this custom key's input, or remove it from the layout.
inputs.remove_custom_bind_description: This custom key button will be removed from the keyboard layout.
inputs.remove_custom_bind_rebinds_warnin: This will also delete {} saved rebind layout entries for this key.
inputs.tooltip.remove_custom_bind: Removes this custom key from the layout. If it has saved rebinds, you'll confirm before deleting them.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_output: Rebind the key fully so both the game action and typed output follow the same target key.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_triggers: Changes the game keybind this key triggers, including non-text actions like Backspace or Home.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_types: Changes what this key types in chat, search boxes, and other in-game text fields.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_types_shift: Changes what this key types while Shift is held in in-game text fields. Leave it unchanged to inherit Types.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_types_shift_caps_l: When enabled, Caps Lock also activates this key's Shift layer in split rebind mode.
inputs.tooltip.rebind_toggle_hotkey: Toggles key rebinding ACTIVE/DISABLED at runtime.
inputs.tooltip.resolve_rebind_targets: When enabled, hotkeys can match the rebind target key for the pressed source key. Disable to only match the actual pressed key.
label.auto_layout_format: Auto layout: X={}  Y={}  Width={}  Height={}
tooltip.confine_cursor: Keeps the mouse cursor inside the game window while in chat, inventory, etc. Not active in this menu.
label.name: Name
label.not_all_rebinds_supported: Not all rebinds supported by Toolscreen are legal for speedrunning
label.note_raw_input: Note: Raw Input must always remain enabled in the game settings
label.tooltip.crop_to: Sets a fixed target size instead of cropping by pixels. The overlay keeps this size even if the source resizes, and won't stretch if the source is smaller.
label.tip_right_click_slider: Tip: Right-click any slider to input a specific value.
mirrors.border_static_shape_overlay: Static shape overlay (set Thickness to 0 to disable)
mirrors.color_picker_help: Hover the preview to inspect pixels. The inset in the top-left shows a magnified view of the hovered pixel. Left-click the preview to save that color into the selected target slot.
mirrors.color_picker_unavailable: No live mirror frame is available yet.
mirrors.delete_group_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete mirror group '{}'?
mirrors.delete_mirror_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete mirror '{}'?
mirrors.name: Name
mirrors.tooltip.color_passthrough: When enabled, matching pixels output their original color instead of the Output Color
mirrors.tooltip.enable_in_group: Enable/disable this mirror in the group
mirrors.tooltip.output_scale: Note: Mirror output settings here do not affect mirrors rendered inside a Mirror Group.
mirrors.tooltip.per_item_sizing:
Width% and Height% only affect mirrors when rendered as part of this group.
Mirrors used outside this group will render at their original size.
mirrors.tooltip.relative_to_screen:
When enabled, position is stored as percentages of screen size.
This makes configs portable across different screen resolutions.
mirrors.warning.reset_mirrors_and_groups:
This will delete ALL custom mirrors and mirror groups
and restore the default mirrors and mirror groups.
modes.change_default_mode_confirm_messag: Are you sure? Your new default mode will be: {}
modes.delete_mode_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete mode '{}'?
modes.eyezoom.delete_overlay_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete overlay '{}'?
modes.eyezoom.tooltip.auto_font_size:
Auto Font Size uses one shared fitted size for every EyeZoom clone label, so all numbers match.
Per-Square Auto Font Size fits each box independently.
Manual Font Size disables auto-fit and uses the slider value.
modes.eyezoom.tooltip.clip_to_zoom_area: When enabled, a manual custom overlay is clipped to the EyeZoom zoom area. Disable it to let the overlay extend outside the zoom area.
modes.eyezoom.tooltip.custom_font: Choose a preset font for EyeZoom overlay text, or switch to Custom for a file path. Leave it on Main GUI font to inherit the global GUI font. Supports TTF, OTF, and TTC files.
modes.eyezoom.tooltip.font_size_clamped: Font size will be clamped to fit inside the overlay boxes.
modes.eyezoom.tooltip.link_rect_to_font: Note: With 'Link Rectangle to Font Size' enabled, box height is still based on Text Font Size.
modes.eyezoom.tooltip.overlay_pixels:
How many colored overlay boxes + numbers to draw on EACH side of the center line.
cloneWidth controls how wide the clone samples; overlayWidth only controls how much of the numbered overlay is drawn.
modes.eyezoom.tooltip.slide_zoom_in: When enabled, the zoom overlay slides in from the left instead of growing with the viewport. Both reach their targets at the same time.
modes.eyezoom.tooltip.use_custom_positio: When enabled, EyeZoom clone output can be moved anywhere on screen using X/Y position instead of being fixed to the left-middle area.
modes.features_remain_functional: Other features (overlays, images, cursors) remain functional.
modes.preemptive.eyezoom_not_found: Note: EyeZoom mode not found; copied resolution display may be stale.
modes.tooltip.enable_border: Draw a border around the game viewport. Border appears outside the game area.
modes.tooltip.override_sensitivity: When enabled, this mode uses its own mouse sensitivity instead of the global setting.
modes.tooltip.sensitivity: Mouse sensitivity for this mode (1.0 = normal)
modes.tooltip.separate_xy_sensitivity: Use different sensitivity values for horizontal (X) and vertical (Y) mouse movement.
modes.tooltip.slide_mirrors_in: When enabled, mirrors slide in from the screen edge they are closest to (left or right) instead of appearing instantly during transitions.
modes.warning.raw_input: WARNING: You have Raw Input disabled. Please enable it in Options -> Controls -> Mouse Settings.
modes.warning.resolution_change:
WARNING: Resolution changing is not supported for Minecraft version {}.{}.{} (requires 1.13+). Mode dimension editing and switching are disabled.
Other features (overlays, images, cursors) remain functional.
ninjabrain.blind_improve_direction: Head {}°, {} blocks away, for better coords
ninjabrain.info_message.combined_certain: Nether coords ({}, {}) have {} chance to hit the stronghold (it is between the top 2 offsets).
ninjabrain.info_message.mc_version: Detected wrong Minecraft version, make sure the correct version is chosen in the settings.
ninjabrain.info_message.mismeasure: Detected unusually large errors, you probably mismeasured or your standard deviation is too low.
ninjabrain.info_message.next_throw_direc: Go left {} blocks, or right {} blocks, for ~95% certainty after next measurement.
ninjabrain.info_message.portal_linking: You might not be able to nether travel into the stronghold due to portal linking.
ninjabrain.hide_if_stale: Hide overlay if it has not updated for a while
ninjabrain.preset_confirm_message: Apply the {} preset to the Ninjabrain overlay? This will replace your current Ninjabrain overlay settings.
ninjabrain.presets_hint: Apply a preset, then tweak the settings below.
ninjabrain.reset_button: Reset Ninjabrain Bot Overlay to Defaults
ninjabrain.reset_confirm: Reset all Ninjabrain Bot overlay settings to defaults?
ninjabrain.show_boat_state_top_bar: Show boat state in top bar
ninjabrain.enable_api_hint: In Ninjabrain Bot, go to Settings -> Advanced -> Enable API.
ninjabrain.failed_line1: Could not determine the stronghold chunk.
ninjabrain.failed_line2: You probably misread one of the eyes.
ninjabrain.status_offline_detail: Could not reach {}. Start Ninjabrain Bot, then go to Settings -> Advanced -> Enable API. Last error: {}
ninjabrain.title: Ninjabrain Bot
ninjabrain.tooltip_always_show: Render the overlay shell even before Ninjabrain Bot has results. Boat state still appears.
ninjabrain.tooltip_only_on_my_screen: When enabled, the Ninjabrain Bot overlay will not be captured by OBS
ninjabrain.tooltip_only_on_obs: When enabled, the Ninjabrain Bot overlay is only visible in OBS, not on your screen
settings.restore_windowed_mode_on_fullsc: Restore windowed mode when exiting fullscreen
settings.debug_passcode_prompt: Enter passcode to access debug options:
settings.enable_verbose_logging: Enable verbose logging for specific subsystems:
settings.capture_fake_cursor_overlay: Render Fake Cursor In OBS/Virtual Camera
settings.debug_mpeg_video_memory_empty: No MPEG video textures are currently queued or uploaded.
settings.debug_mpeg_video_memory_note: VRAM counts uploaded RGBA8 video textures currently kept resident on the GPU.
settings.tooltip.colorspace:
Controls how mirror color matching interprets the captured pixels.
Applies globally to all mirrors.

Auto: tries both sRGB-space and linear-space matching and uses the better match.
Assume sRGB: converts sampled pixels + target colors to linear for matching.
Assume Linear: treats sampled pixels as linear; converts only target colors.
settings.tooltip.capture_fake_cursor: Draws the current active game cursor texture into the OBS/Virtual Camera capture output without adding it to your own screen.
settings.tooltip.delay_rendering_until_f: Calls glFinish() before SwapBuffers to ensure all GPU rendering is complete before presenting.
settings.tooltip.fake_cursor: Renders a fake cursor overlay at the current mouse position for debugging.
settings.tooltip.limit_capture_framerate: When enabled, Toolscreen updates OBS capture at half the detected OBS sampling rate, but never below 60 fps.
settings.tooltip.profiler_scale:
Scale of the profiler overlay
25% = tiny, 50% = half size, 100% = normal, 200% = double size
settings.tooltip.restore_windowed_mode_o: When enabled, Toolscreen re-centers the game window to its windowed restore size after the game leaves a monitor-sized fullscreen or borderless state.
settings.tooltip.video_cache_budget_mib:
Maximum memory budget used when loading MPEG videos.
If a video would need more than this amount, Toolscreen skips loading that video entirely.
Set to Disabled to skip all MPEG video loads.
settings.tooltip.show_texture_grid: Displays a grid of all game OpenGL textures on screen for debugging.
settings.tooltip.virtual_camera:
Outputs the game, including overlays, to the OBS Virtual Camera.
You can use this to screenshare in Discord, or to capture in OBS.

Requires OBS Virtual Camera driver to be installed.
Works independently of OBS being open.
supporters.donate: If you'd like to support, please consider donating at
supporters.thanks: Thanks to these people for supporting the development of Toolscreen!
tooltip.auto_borderless: Automatically puts Minecraft in borderless mode when the window is detected on startup.
tooltip.capture_interaction:
When enabled, clicking on this overlay while the cursor is visible
will focus it and forward mouse/keyboard inputs to the real window.
Click outside the overlay or press Escape to unfocus.
tooltip.capture_method:
Windows 10+: Captures most windows (recommended)
  - Similar to the "Windows 10" capture mode in OBS

BitBlt: Captures from window device context, less performant
  - Only recommended if Windows 10+ method doesn't work
tooltip.capture_search_interval:
How often to search for the window if it's not found (in seconds).
Lower values find windows faster but use more CPU.
Recommended: 1.0s (1 second)
tooltip.cursor_change: When enabled, the mouse cursor will change based on the current game state.
tooltip.font: Search bundled or Windows fonts, or switch to Custom for any .ttf, .otf, or .ttc file. Changes apply immediately.
tooltip.gui_font_scale: Scales the main Toolscreen GUI font without changing overall widget sizing. Changes apply immediately.
tooltip.fps_limit.advanced:
Limits the game's maximum frame rate.
Not quite as precise as NVIDIA Control Panel but close enough.
Lower FPS can reduce GPU load and power consumption.
tooltip.hide_animations_in_game:
When enabled, mode transitions appear instant on your screen,
but OBS Game Capture will show the animations.
tooltip.upload_debug_info: Combines the current Toolscreen log, the current Minecraft latest.log from INST_MC_DIR/logs/latest.log, and the current effective config.toml into one upload, then copies the share link to the clipboard.
tooltip.mouse_sensitivity:
Multiplies mouse movement for raw input events (mouselook).
1.0 = normal sensitivity, higher = faster, lower = slower.
Useful for adjusting mouse speed when using stretched resolutions.
tooltip.open_config_folder: Open the Toolscreen folder that contains config.toml.
tooltip.open_cursor_folder: Open or create the Toolscreen cursors folder for .cur and .ico files.
tooltip.toggle_borderless: Toggles the game window between its previous windowed size and a borderless, monitor-sized window.
tooltip.toggle_image_overlays.advanced: Toggles visibility of all Image Overlays (does not change your mode config).
tooltip.toggle_image_overlays.basic: Toggles the visibility of all Image Overlays.
tooltip.toggle_ninjabrain_overlay.advanc: Toggles visibility of the Ninjabrain overlay (does not change your Ninjabrain overlay settings).
tooltip.toggle_ninjabrain_overlay.basic: Toggles visibility of the Ninjabrain overlay.
tooltip.toggle_window_overlay.advanced:
Toggles visibility of all Window Overlays (does not change your mode config).
When hidden, overlay interaction forwarding is also disabled.
tooltip.toggle_window_overlays.basic: Toggles visibility of all Window Overlays. When hidden, interaction forwarding is disabled.
tooltip.view_licenses: View license information for open-source libraries used in this project.
tooltip.windows_mouse_speed:
Temporarily overrides Windows mouse speed setting while game is running.
0 = Disabled (use system setting)
1-20 = Override Windows mouse speed (10 = default Windows speed)
Affects cursor movement in game menus. Original setting is restored on exit.
tooltip.let_cursor_escape_window: For pre 1.13, prevents the cursor being locked to the game window when in fullscreen.
transition.note.fullscreen_background: Note: Fullscreen has no background. Transitions use the other mode's background.
transition.tooltip.relative_stretching:
When enabled, viewport-relative overlays scale with the viewport during animation.
When disabled, overlays move with the viewport but keep their original size.
transition.tooltip.skip_x_animation: When enabled, the X axis (width) instantly jumps to target while Y animates.
transition.tooltip.skip_y_animation: When enabled, the Y axis (height) instantly jumps to target while X animates.
window.overlays_match_priority_title_exe: Match title, otherwise find window of same executable
window.overlays_thread_exit: Window capture thread may not be running.
window.overlays_tooltip_border: Draw a border around the window overlay.
window.overlays_tooltip_force_update:
When enabled, Toolscreen sends redraw messages to the target window
on every capture frame (RedrawWindow + WM_PAINT + WM_CAPTURECHANGED).
Useful for windows that stop updating while in the background.
window.overlays_warning_reset_to_default: This will delete ALL window overlays.
browser.overlays_warning_reset_to_defaul: This will delete ALL browser overlays.
browser.tooltip.only_on_my_screen: When enabled, this browser overlay will only be visible to you and not captured by OBS
browser.tooltip.custom_css: Inject CSS into this overlay's top-level page. Changes apply live and on future navigations.
browser.tooltip.hardware_acceleration: When enabled, this overlay uses WebView2 hardware acceleration. Disable it if the browser source has compatibility issues on your system; the overlay will recreate automatically.
browser.tooltip.allow_system_media_keys: When enabled, this overlay can respond to your keyboard's system media keys such as play/pause and next/previous track.
browser.tooltip.reload_interval: How often to refresh the page automatically. Set to 0 to disable timed refreshes.
browser.tooltip.transparent_background: Uses transparent WebView capture, which is slower. Leave this off for the faster opaque capture path, or pair the opaque mode with color keys if you need to punch out a solid background color.
browser.tooltip_delete:
Are you sure you want to delete browser overlay '{}' ?
This cannot be undone.
window.tooltip_delete:
Are you sure you want to delete window overlay '{}' ?
This cannot be undone.
window.tooltip_match_priority:
Window title must match: Only captures windows with exact matching title
Match title, otherwise find window of same executable: Prefers matching title, falls back to same executable (e.g chrome.exe)
window.tooltip.only_on_my_screen: When enabled, this image will only be visible to you and not captured by OBS
profiles.manage_hint: Switch profiles from the left, then edit the active profile's name, color, and override sections here.
profiles.sections_hint: Checked sections are stored in this profile. Unchecked sections stay shared in config.toml.
